title: Johnson & Johnson — Contacts
html:
Module 4: J&J Contact Map & Relationship Intelligence

Governing Thought

The J&J Oncology Insights organization is a 30+ person stakeholder network split across three pillars — Solid Tumor (Lia Zepeda), Heme (Katy Hu), and Medical Insights (Alia Jafry) — all reporting up to Kevin O'Neill, VP US Insights & Analytics. Your day-to-day work will flow through 6-8 Associate Directors and Managers who own specific brands (Mandy on Lung, Erin on Prostate, Christina on Bladder, Lynn/Meku on MM, Molly/James on Bispecifics, Melissa/Kazim on Carvykti). Directors set priorities and approve scope; Senior Directors and Kevin control budget. Beyond Insights, a ring of 15+ adjacent stakeholders — sales leadership, field medical, marketing, and CI — attend readouts, challenge data, and influence renewals. Winning on this account means building trust at the Associate Director level while creating visibility at Director+ through sharp, actionable deliverables. This document maps every name, email, reporting line, and working-style note you need.

J&J Insights Organization Structure

Kevin O'Neill — VP, US Insights & Analytics, JJIM
  EA: Clea Armenti
  |
  |--- Lia Zepeda — Sr. Director, Solid Tumor
  |      |--- Larry Lee — Director, Lung & Precision Medicine
  |      |      |--- Mandy Merrill — Assoc. Director, Lung (Rybrevant/Lazcluze)
  |      |--- Ryan Catania — Director, Prostate
  |      |      |--- Erin Lennon — Assoc. Director, Prostate (Erleada)
  |      |--- Praveen Advani — Director, Bladder (Inlexzo/Balversa)
  |             |--- Christina Maloney — Assoc. Director, Bladder (Inlexzo)
  |
  |--- Katy Hu — Sr. Director, Heme Onc & Oncology Franchise
  |      |--- Paul DiPietro — Director, Darzalex & MM Portfolio
  |      |      |--- Lynn Adams — Manager, Darzalex & MM Portfolio
  |      |      |--- Inna Lurie — Assoc. Director, Darzalex
  |      |      |--- Meku Ayele — Manager, MM Portfolio
  |      |--- Rick Dunkelberger — Director, Bispecifics (Tec/Tal)
  |      |      |--- Molly Telford — Assoc. Director, Bispecifics
  |      |      |--- James Scott — Manager, Bispecifics
  |      |--- Kyla Metzger — Director, CAR-T
  |      |      |--- Melissa Rosenberg — Assoc. Director, Carvykti
  |      |      |--- Kazim Ali — Manager, Carvykti
  |      |--- John Fezzuoglio ("Fezz") — Director, Oncology Franchise, PECS
  |             |--- Katie Szelc — Assoc. Director, Oncology Franchise, PECS
  |
  |--- Alia Jafry — Sr. Director, US Medical Insights
         |--- Heather Kulesik — Director, Medical Insights
         |--- Payal Shankar — Assoc. Director, Medical Insights (loosely IMM)
         |--- Ben Ringel — Assoc. Director, Medical Insights (loosely Heme)

Tier 1: Day-to-Day Contacts (Must Know Immediately)

These are the people you will interact with weekly. Learn their names, working styles, and brand responsibilities before your first call.

Solid Tumor — Lung

Name Title Email Working Style
Mandy Merrill Assoc. Director, Lung (Rybrevant/Lazcluze) amerril1@its.jnj.com Leans traditional MR ("here is the data" not "here is what it means"). Sends interesting questions but hasn't pushed something new on Lung yet. Sometimes needs a reminder ping. Will NOT own Rybrevant expansion into Head & Neck / GI.

Solid Tumor — Prostate

Name Title Email Working Style
Erin Lennon Assoc. Director, Prostate (Erleada) elennon1@its.jnj.com 10+ years at J&J (previously Neuroscience, before that BMS sales). Focus skews to personal promotion; very little interest in NPP. Meets with ZoomRx 1x/month. Very interested in SFTO but needs to convince analytics side on cost.

Solid Tumor — Bladder

Name Title Email Working Style
Christina Maloney Assoc. Director, Bladder (Inlexzo) cmalon12@its.jnj.com High stress but knows brand well. Will not always remember prior discussions — recap key points at start of calls. Has tech issues with filedrop — use SharePoint instead. Consumer background. Growing scope as future indications come online.

Heme — MM Portfolio

Name Title Email Working Style
Lynn Adams Manager, Darzalex & MM Portfolio LAdams14@its.jnj.com Day-to-day contact for MM PET and MM OCE. Growing in PMR confidence. Thoughtful about getting buy-in across brands. Appreciates suggestions (e.g., new question wording).
Meku Ayele Manager, MM Portfolio MAyele@its.jnj.com Joined MM team this year. Analytics skew, less champion of PMR. Sometimes needs reminders for what ZoomRx can do.

Heme — Bispecifics

Name Title Email Working Style
Molly Telford Assoc. Director, Bispecifics (Tec/Tal) mtelfor1@its.jnj.com Detail-oriented. Focuses on stat sig differences for comparisons. Grown in responsibility and confidence since joining.
James Scott Manager, Bispecifics (Tec/Tal) JScott46@its.jnj.com Recently moved from Lung/Rybrevant team. Splits time PMR and Analytics. Pushes insights to actionability — doesn't shy from recommendations. Advocate for ZoomRx — will promote capabilities to others. Believer in MSL and OCE work, not just commercial tracking.

Heme — Carvykti

Name Title Email Working Style
Melissa Rosenberg Assoc. Director, Carvykti mrosenb9@ITS.JNJ.com Balances J&J needs with Legend co-promote partners. PET funded through Legend. ZoomRx previously provided pricing but never heard back despite multiple pings — follow up.
Kazim Ali Manager, Carvykti KAli14@ITS.JNJ.com New to Heme but knows ZoomRx from IMM FRM work. Focused on accelerating Carvykti growth via insights. Open to new methods/ideas.

Cross-Brand / Franchise

Name Title Email Working Style
Katie Szelc Assoc. Director, Oncology Franchise, PECS kszelc@ITS.JNJ.com KAM tracking is her "baby." Does NOT include ZoomRx "in the room" for internal readouts. Protective about work until done. Calls can be chaotic. Responsible for J&J Oncology MR SOPs. Starting AI explorations. Mentioned thinking about life outside J&J post March bonus.
Devon Brown Assoc. Director, Insights & Analytics, PECS FRM DBrow132@its.jnj.com FRM lead for IMM and ONC. Not a lot of PMR experience — likes ZoomRx suggestions/guidance. Open to supplemental work ideas.

Medical Insights

Name Title Email Working Style
Payal Shankar Assoc. Director, Medical Insights (loosely IMM) pshanka2@its.jnj.com Responsible for MSL work + dotted line to IMM. Will call directly with last-minute questions. Looking for ZoomRx to be a partner, especially on calls with leadership. Meshes well with Nick not Gates. Good to offer to come in person.

Tier 2: Decision Makers & Influencers

These are Directors and Senior Directors who approve scope, set budgets, and escalate (or kill) projects. You need credibility with them, even if you don't meet weekly.

Senior Leadership

Name Title Email What They Care About
Kevin O'Neill VP, US Insights & Analytics, JJIM KOneill6@its.jnj.com All Insights & Analytics for North America across all pharma TAs. Previously tasked Praveen with cross-J&J PET approach. May be more focused on analytics than PMR. EA: Clea Armenti (carmenti@its.jnj.com).
Lia Zepeda Sr. Director, Solid Tumor lzepeda@its.jnj.com All solid tumor commercial insights report to her. Will ask questions on sample size and sample makeup.
Katy Hu Sr. Director, Heme Onc & Oncology Franchise khu@its.jnj.com Little direct interaction with ZoomRx but work reaches her — especially cross-brand (KAM, FRM). Many stakeholders report up to her. Good to build rapport / get more face time.
Alia Jafry Sr. Director, US Medical Insights ajafry@its.jnj.com Leads Medical Insights team. Sometimes Horsham, mostly Titusville. Previously MM commercial side.

Directors

Name Title Email What They Care About
Larry Lee Director, Lung & Precision Medicine LLee16@its.jnj.com Came back to Oncology/Lung when Gianno Saiter left to Merck. Gives directs a lot of leeway but is involved in decisions. Mandy reports to him.
Ryan Catania Director, Prostate rcatania@its.jnj.com Joined this year after Heme/Solid split. Has pushed Erin to pull new insights. Will fill in for Erin when OOO. Trying to bridge PMR and analytics sides.
Praveen Advani Director, Bladder (Inlexzo/Balversa) padvani@its.jnj.com Knows ZoomRx work well. Previously oversaw all solid tumor before the split. Spearheaded cross-J&J PET idea (put on back burner by leadership). Likes clear, simple visualizations and scorecard slides. Values benchmark data. "If Prostate gets something, we need it too."
Paul DiPietro Director, Darzalex & MM Portfolio pdipietr@ITS.JNJ.com Likes ZoomRx work, has elevated to leadership. Lets Lynn/Meku handle day-to-day. Knew ZoomRx from prior role involved with OCE/FRM combo pilot.
Rick Dunkelberger Director, Bispecifics (Tec/Tal) RDunkelb@ITS.JNJ.com Often pushes for "what does this mean" / "what is the impact." Concerns with data quality prevent SFTO moving forward.
Kyla Metzger Director, CAR-T kmetzger@its.jnj.com Little interaction but joined FRM readout. Previously cc'd on OCE readouts.
John Fezzuoglio ("Fezz") Director, Oncology Franchise, PECS JFezzuog@its.jnj.com Legacy relationship with Dave from past roles. Covers cross-Oncology bigger picture questions. Gives interesting insight on Oncology leadership priorities but doesn't always translate to projects sold. Evolving budget situation.
Heather Kulesik Director, Medical Insights hkulisek@its.jnj.com Reports to Alia. Previously oncology to neuroscience to this role. Focused on sample size and on-list percentage. Needs help explaining methodology rationale to stakeholders. Often mentions NPS study — wants separate MSL tracking for more detail.

Other Influencers in Insights

Name Title Email Context
Inna Lurie Assoc. Director, Darzalex ilurie@ITS.JNJ.com Moved from Imbruvica to MM. Was briefly very involved then stepped back when Lynn came on.
Ben Ringel Assoc. Director, Medical Insights (loosely Heme) bringel@its.jnj.com Only involved advising for MSL tracking. Could be good to understand more about his role.

Tier 3: Adjacent Stakeholders

These people do not commission ZoomRx work directly, but they attend readouts, challenge findings, influence renewals, and shape how insights land internally. Knowing their names and roles prevents surprises.

Sales & Commercial Leadership

Name Title Email Context
Bill Faxon National Sales Director, Hematology wfaxon@its.jnj.com Challenges data based on sample size. Molly will prep ahead of readouts.
Micah Chiles Regional Business Director, Lung mchiles@its.jnj.com Previously Group Product Director for HCP Professional Promotion. Vocal during readouts. 3 years at McKinsey.
Mike Cavalcante Head of Sales, Lung Cancer Franchise Mcavalc3@its.jnj.com Sales leadership, active in readouts.
Cynthia Accuosti Jones VP, Oncology Sales & Marketing, Lung caccuost@its.jnj.com Oversees all Lung commercial. Invited to readouts but often can't join.
Roya Motarjemi Regional Business Director, Lung, West rmotarje@its.jnj.com Previously district manager. Very active in readouts.
Biljana Naumovic President, US Oncology Solid Tumor bnaumovi@its.jnj.com Invited to 1 meeting (weekly "Flight Team" for lung). Leverage above-brand projects to get on her radar.

Marketing & Portfolio

Name Title Email Context
Mary Mettias Group Marketing Director, US Oncology Portfolio mmettias@its.jnj.com Main KAM stakeholder for Katie. Opinionated during fielding.
Dominique Thibault Product Director, Consumer Activation & Patient Experience, Lung dthibau1@its.jnj.com Triggered social listening/Reddit proposal.
Abby Levine / Katie Meehan Assoc. Director, Commercial Insights & Strategy, Prostate (job share) alevine2@its.jnj.com / kmeehan8@its.jnj.com Erleada analytics. Serve as roadblock for SFTO. Connected to ZS dashboard team.

Field Medical & MSL

Name Title Email Context
Kellie Ward Head, Solid Tumor Medical Excellence kward7@its.jnj.com Led Lung MSLs while Geeta Solanki on leave. Now also leads new product MSLs (incl. Head & Neck). Has detailed usable quote on MSL work value.
Geeta Solanki National MSL Head, Lung gsolank1@its.jnj.com Works with Kellie Ward. Initiated and got MSL solid tumor work running. Appreciates tracking showing team impact.
Reese Garcia Burant Assoc. Director, Insights Solid Tumor MSL rgarc210@its.jnj.com Supports Kellie Ward. Helps with admin like getting survey HCC approved.
John Skrabal Head GU Field Medical Group jskrabal@its.jnj.com Responsible for GU MSLs. Likes tracking but scattered and hard to pin down. Leverage Reese and Kellie.
Jacquelyn Keehne-Miron National Director, Oncology Clinical Education jkeehnem@its.jnj.com Responsible for OCE teams. Likes good data. Will get confused and call ZoomRx "the ZS team."
Brandee Pappalardo VP, US Medical Affairs, Immunology bpappala@its.jnj.com Leads PsO and PsA MSLs. Very opinionated on study/sample setup.
Chris Gasink VP, US Medical Affairs, Gastro and Autoantibody cgasink@its.jnj.com Co-part to Brandee. Sees value in MSL study setup.
Jennifer Grush Director, MSL Strategy & Operations Jgrush@its.jnj.com Supports Brandee and Chris in role similar to Kellie Ward's.
Holly Hinkle Director, Field Org Strategy & Training Solutions hhinkle1@its.jnj.com Operational enablement for FRMs. Key stakeholder for Devon on FRM work.

Data, CI & Analytics

Name Title Email Context
Igor Rudychev Head of Data Strategy, Platforms, Partnerships & Innovations irudyche@its.jnj.com Responsible for data strategy across JJIM. Friendship with Brian pre-ZoomRx. Long pharma tenure (Pfizer, AZ, Novartis, Amgen). Current headaches: real-time market share data, consistency/noise issues.
Jesse Heap Sr. Director, Customer AI Products & Strategy — Leading effort to build AI tool for call plans. Unknown if reports to Igor or parallel.
Brittany Carroll Global Director, Marketing Insights & Analytics, Leukemia/Lymphoma/Cell Therapy Bcarrol5@its.jnj.com Main Ferma Congress contact. Recently promoted — unclear if now includes global PMR or still CI.
Sahnila Enam-Varughese Director, Business Intelligence, Oncology senamvar@its.jnj.com CI for oncology teams.
Faith Poelker US Oncology & Portfolio CI fpoelker@its.jnj.com CI for oncology.
Diana Gowe Director, Global Strategic Analytics — Limited interaction noted.
Jennifer Wheeler IMM Medical Affairs Analytics jwheel12@its.jnj.com Supports IMM medical affairs data. Previously on oncology side.

Relationship Intelligence

Working Style Do's and Don'ts

DO:

  • Recap context at the start of calls with Christina Maloney — she will not always remember prior discussions
  • Use SharePoint for file sharing with Christina (filedrop causes tech issues)
  • Send reminder pings to Mandy Merrill and Meku Ayele — both sometimes need a nudge to move things forward
  • Offer to come in person for Payal Shankar, especially for leadership calls
  • Prep Molly Telford before any readout that Bill Faxon will attend — he challenges sample size
  • Lead with "what it means" for Rick Dunkelberger — he pushes for impact, not just data
  • Provide benchmark data and scorecard slides for Praveen Advani — he values simple, comparative visuals
  • Proactively suggest question wording and approaches for Lynn Adams and Devon Brown — both appreciate guidance
  • Pair Nick (not Gates) with Payal Shankar — known chemistry match

DON'T:

  • Don't expect Katie Szelc to include ZoomRx in internal readouts — she is protective about work until it is finished
  • Don't assume Erin Lennon cares about NPP — her focus is personal promotion almost exclusively
  • Don't present to Mandy in a "here is what it means" style unless asked — she leans traditional MR and prefers "here is the data"
  • Don't send filedrop links to Christina Maloney — use SharePoint
  • Don't ignore the Carvykti pricing follow-up — ZoomRx provided pricing to Melissa Rosenberg and never heard back despite multiple pings
  • Don't call ZoomRx "ZS" around Jacquelyn Keehne-Miron — she already confuses the two; reinforce the ZoomRx brand

Meeting Cadences

Cadence Contacts
Weekly standing meetings Core insights contacts (Mandy, Erin, Christina, Lynn, Molly, James, Katie Szelc, Payal)
Biweekly / Monthly Secondary contacts (Directors: Larry, Ryan, Praveen, Paul, Rick, Heather; Franchise: Fezz, Devon)
Monthly Erin Lennon (1x/month with ZoomRx)
Ad hoc Leadership (Kevin, Lia, Katy Hu, Alia) and field contacts (Kellie, Geeta, Micah, Mike C.)

Relationship Building Priorities for FY26

High Priority — Invest Now

Contact Why
Katy Hu Work reaches her but little direct interaction. Getting face time builds air cover for Heme renewals and cross-brand work (KAM, FRM). She controls budget gravity for half the portfolio.
Ryan Catania New to role, actively trying to bridge PMR and analytics. A natural ally — if you help him prove PMR value to the analytics side, it unlocks SFTO for Prostate.
Kazim Ali New to Heme but already knows ZoomRx from IMM FRM. Open to new methods. Investing early shapes how Carvykti insights work develops.
James Scott ZoomRx advocate who promotes capabilities to others. Recently moved to Bispecifics — keep the relationship warm so he evangelizes from his new seat.
Payal Shankar Wants ZoomRx as a true partner, especially on leadership calls. In-person visits and responsiveness will deepen this into a champion relationship.

Medium Priority — Maintain and Deepen

Contact Why
Praveen Advani Long ZoomRx history, knows the work. Keep feeding him benchmarks and scorecards. His "if Prostate gets it, we need it too" instinct is a reliable upsell trigger.
Lynn Adams Growing in PMR confidence. Continued mentorship-style guidance (suggesting questions, framing) cements ZoomRx as her go-to partner.
Molly Telford Growing in responsibility. Stat sig rigor means she will defend ZoomRx work internally if the data holds.
Devon Brown FRM lead who actively wants ZoomRx guidance. Low-hanging fruit for supplemental work.
Heather Kulesik Needs help explaining methodology to stakeholders — if you arm her well, she becomes an internal advocate for MSL tracking expansion.

Watch List — Monitor for Opportunity

Contact Why
Katie Szelc Mentioned thinking about life outside J&J post March bonus. If she leaves, KAM tracking and MR SOPs will need a new owner — be ready for the transition.
Inna Lurie Stepped back when Lynn came on. Could re-engage if MM scope expands or Lynn moves.
Biljana Naumovic President, US Oncology Solid Tumor. Continue leveraging above-brand projects to get on her radar. Any visibility here elevates ZoomRx's strategic positioning.
Igor Rudychev Head of Data with headaches on real-time market share and data consistency. If ZoomRx can address noise/consistency issues, this opens a non-PMR revenue stream.
Jesse Heap Building AI tool for call plans. If this intersects with ZoomRx OCE/FRM data, could become a partnership opportunity.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

Relationship Intelligence
Working Style Do's and Don'ts
DO:

Recap context at the start of calls with Christina Maloney — she will not always remember prior discussions
Use SharePoint for file sharing with Christina (filedrop causes tech issues)
Send reminder pings to Mandy Merrill and Meku Ayele — both sometimes need a nudge to move things forward
Offer to come in person for Payal Shankar, especially for leadership calls
Prep Molly Telford before any readout that Bill Faxon will attend — he challenges sample size
Lead with "what it means" for Rick Dunkelberger — he pushes for impact, not just data
Provide benchmark data and scorecard slides for Praveen Advani — he values simple, comparative visuals
Proactively suggest question wording and approaches for Lynn Adams and Devon Brown — both appreciate guidance
Pair Nick (not Gates) with Payal Shankar — known chemistry match

DON'T:

Don't expect Katie Szelc to include ZoomRx in internal readouts — she is protective about work until it is finished
Don't assume Erin Lennon cares about NPP — her focus is personal promotion almost exclusively
Don't present to Mandy in a "here is what it means" style unless asked — she leans traditional MR and prefers "here is the data"
Don't send filedrop links to Christina Maloney — use SharePoint
Don't ignore the Carvykti pricing follow-up — ZoomRx provided pricing to Melissa Rosenberg and never heard back despite multiple pings
Don't call ZoomRx "ZS" around Jacquelyn Keehne-Miron — she already confuses the two; reinforce the ZoomRx brand
